questions.question: Which geographical factor mainly helped India establish early trade contacts with other civilizations?
questions.explanation: India’s central location in Asia helped it establish trade contacts with many civilizations.

questions.question: Which physical feature protects India from the cold winds of Central Asia?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas act as a natural barrier protecting India from cold winds.

questions.question: Why is India considered a subcontinent?

options.A: It is located on an island
options.C: Because of its large size and diverse geography
options.D: Because it lies near the Equator

questions.explanation: India’s large size and diverse geographical features make it a subcontinent.

questions.question: Which factor mainly influenced the spread of Indian culture abroad?
questions.explanation: Trade routes allowed exchange of goods, ideas and culture.

questions.question: Why does India follow a single standard time despite its large east-west extent?
questions.explanation: A single time zone ensures administrative convenience across the country.

questions.question: Which feature of India helped in the development of maritime trade?
questions.explanation: India's peninsular shape extends into the Indian Ocean facilitating trade.

questions.question: Which factor contributed most to India's strategic importance in Asia?
questions.explanation: India’s central position connects many regions.

questions.question: Which geographical feature allowed traders to enter India from Central Asia?
questions.explanation: Mountain passes across the Himalayas allowed travellers to enter India.

questions.question: Which sea route historically connected India with Europe and Africa?
questions.explanation: The Indian Ocean route connected India with Europe and Africa.

questions.question: Which Indian innovation spread widely through trade routes?
questions.explanation: The decimal system spread from India to other parts of the world.

questions.question: Which neighbouring country lies across the Palk Strait?
questions.explanation: Sri Lanka lies across the Palk Strait.

questions.question: Which island group strengthens India's maritime security?
questions.explanation: The Andaman and Nicobar Islands lie near important sea routes.

questions.question: Which geographical feature contributes most to India's cultural diversity?
questions.explanation: India’s large geographical size leads to diverse cultures and climates.

questions.question: Which of the following helped India maintain ancient trade relations?
questions.explanation: All these factors helped India's trade relations.

questions.question: Which geographical feature influenced India's climatic conditions significantly?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas influence India's climate.

questions.question: Which factor mainly contributed to the growth of ancient Indian civilization?
questions.explanation: Trade and cultural contacts helped ancient civilization grow.

questions.question: Which location gives India an advantage in global communication?

options.A: Central position between East and West

questions.explanation: India lies centrally between East and West Asia.

questions.question: Which neighbouring country lies south of Lakshadweep Islands?
questions.explanation: Maldives lies south of Lakshadweep.

questions.question: Which natural feature forms India's northern boundary?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas form the northern boundary.

questions.question: Which geographical factor helps India maintain strong trade relations?
questions.explanation: The peninsular extension aids maritime trade.

questions.question: Which feature makes India strategically important in South Asia?
questions.explanation: India's central location provides strategic advantage.

questions.question: Which factor helped spread Indian literature abroad?
questions.explanation: Trade routes helped spread Indian literature.

questions.question: Which geographical element connects India with Southeast Asia?
questions.explanation: The Bay of Bengal connects India with Southeast Asia.

questions.question: Which region helped India maintain maritime trade with West Asia?
questions.explanation: The Arabian Sea helped maintain trade with West Asia.

questions.question: Which factor best explains India's importance in global trade historically?
questions.explanation: India’s central location and sea routes made it an important trade centre.
